column2: Question
column3: Answer
column4: Source_Note_or_URL

column2: Hey, I'm thinking of applying for a resident parking permit in Amsterdam. Is it a must for me to be officially registered at my Amsterdam address?
column3: Yes, you must meet certain conditions, and one of these conditions is that you are registered at the address for which you are applying for the permit.
column4: URL1 (from "You must meet these conditions" section)

column2: What's the rule about car ownership for the resident parking permit? Does the car *have* to be in my name?
column3: The car should be registered in your name, or you need to be the only user of the lease, hire, or company car for which you are applying.
column4: URL1 (from "You must meet these conditions" section)

column2: My car has foreign license plates. Can I still apply for a resident parking permit in Amsterdam, or is it only for Dutch cars?
column3: Yes, the vehicle may also have a non-Dutch registration number.
column4: URL1 (from "You must meet these conditions" section)

column2: I've heard there are some environmental rules for cars. If I have a diesel car, what emission standard does it need to meet for a parking permit?
column3: For diesel cars, your vehicle must meet emission standard 5 or higher.
column4: URL1 (from "Your vehicle must meet the environmental requirements" section)

column2: I'm looking at moving into a brand-new apartment building in Amsterdam. Will I be able to get a resident parking permit there?
column3: Parking permits are never available for new-build projects. This also applies to large-scale renovation projects.
column4: URL1 (mentions "new-build projects (in Dutch)") & URL4 (confirms "Bij nieuwbouwprojecten...geven wij geen parkeervergunningen meer af")

column2: What if I already have a garage or a place to park at my home? Can I still apply for a resident parking permit?
column3: You cannot already have an available parking space. You also cannot apply for a parking permit if you have, or have had in the past, the possibility of buying or renting your own parking space. Own parking space(s) are deducted from the maximum number of permits you can get. If the outcome is zero, you will not receive a parking permit.
column4: URL1 (from "In some parts of Amsterdam..." section and "Parking space at your house" section)

column2: I have a lease car directly from a leasing company. What kind of documents would I need to show when applying for the permit?
column3: If you lease or rent the car directly from a leasing or rental company, you have to scan and upload a copy of the lease contract or agreement (maximum of 1 year old), or a copy of the declaration of the lease (maximum of 1 month old).
column4: URL1 (from "A lease car or a rental car" section under "What you need to apply")

column2: How do I actually start the application process online for the resident parking permit?
column3: To apply online, you log in with DigiD. You'll then fill in a number of details.
column4: URL1 (from "Apply" section)

column2: I'd prefer to apply by mail for the parking permit. Is that possible, and what's the first thing I need to do?
column3: Yes, you can use a paper form to apply. First, you'll need to collect all the necessary documents and make copies, then print the application form for residents’ parking permits (PDF, 63 KB) and complete it.
column4: URL2 (from "Here’s how it works" section)

column2: My car is in the garage for repairs for a few weeks, and I've got a loaner car from them. Do I need to do anything about my parking permit?
column3: If you get a new car, or temporarily drive another car, you need to notify the municipality of the change of registration number. For a loan or rental car from a garage, you'll typically need a loan or rental agreement on official letterhead with details like the date, signature by the garage, your name, registration number of the loan car and your own car, and the reason (repair or damage).
column4: URL3 (from "Temporary change of registration number" and introductory paragraph)

column2: Hey, I want to apply for a resident permit, how do I do it? (Covers also "Hello I help for parking permit" and "Can you help me applying for a resident parking permit?")
column3: You have two main ways to apply for a resident parking permit: 1. Online: Log in with your DigiD and fill in the details. 2. By post: Collect your necessary documents, print and complete the official application form, and then mail it.
column4: URL1 ("Apply" section) & URL2 ("Here’s how it works" section)

column2: Hello, can you help me with everything I need if I want to apply for a parking permit as resident?
column3: Generally, to get a resident parking permit in Amsterdam, you'll need to: 1. Be officially registered at an address in Amsterdam. 2. Ensure your vehicle (which can have foreign plates) meets environmental standards (e.g., diesel cars need emission standard 5+). 3. The vehicle should typically be in your name, or you're the sole user if it's leased or a company car. 4. Be aware that permits aren't usually available for new-builds, and having existing parking can affect eligibility. 5. Gather specific documents based on your car ownership (own, lease, company). 6. You can then apply online using DigiD or by post using a paper form.
column4: Synthesized from URL1, URL2, URL4 & previous Q&A

column2: What specific things do I need for an Amsterdam Resident parking permit?
column3: For an Amsterdam Resident parking permit, you primarily need to: be officially registered at the Amsterdam address for the permit; have a vehicle registered in your name or be the sole user of a lease/company car; ensure your car meets current environmental standards (e.g., emission standard 5 or higher for diesel cars); and generally, you cannot already have an available parking space. You'll also need specific documents depending on your car's ownership status.
column4: URL1 (various sections: "You must meet these conditions", "environmental requirements", "Parking space at your house", "What you need to apply")

column2: I will move to Amsterdam in July. What will I need to do if I want to apply for a parking permit once I'm there?
column3: Once you move to Amsterdam in July and are officially registered at your new Amsterdam address, that registration is the first key requirement. After that, you'll need to ensure your car meets the city's rules for ownership (e.g., in your name or a sole-user lease/company car) and the environmental standards. Then you can proceed with the application, usually online with DigiD or by post.
column4: URL1 ("You must meet these conditions" section & "Apply" section)

column2: What do I need if I want a parking permit or waiver for a blue zone?
column3: For blue zones, you typically apply for a 'blue zone waiver' or 'ontheffing.' The requirements depend on your situation, such as whether you're a resident, a company, or receiving informal care. For residents, you generally need a car registered in your name (or a lease/company car) and be registered at an address in the blue zone area. Specific documents, like a lease agreement for a lease car, would be needed. The cost for a waiver in 2025 is €174.80.
column4: URL5 (from scraped_parking_permit_text.txt, section on "Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen")

column2: I need a resident parking permit but somehow I can't apply for it, or my application might fail. Can you help me understand why?
column3: Common reasons you might not be able to get a resident parking permit, or why an application might not proceed, include: not being officially registered at the Amsterdam address for which you're applying; your vehicle not meeting the required environmental standards; issues with the vehicle's registration or lease/company car documentation; if you live in a new-build project (as permits are often not available for these); or if you already have access to a private parking space. It's important to check all these conditions on the municipality's website.
column4: URL1 (various sections: "You must meet these conditions", "environmental requirements", "new-build projects", "Parking space at your house") & URL4

column2: I'm trying to gather my paperwork for the resident parking permit. Can you give me a complete list of every document I might need?
column3: The specific documents you need for a resident parking permit depend on your individual situation. For example, if the vehicle is registered in your name, often no extra documents are needed for that. However, for a lease car, you'd need the lease contract. For a company car, you'd need things like a payslip and a declaration from your employer. It's best to check the official Amsterdam municipality website for the detailed list corresponding to whether it's your own vehicle, a lease car, a company car, or if you have special circumstances. This can be found here: https://www.amsterdam.nl/en/parking/apply-resident-parking-permit/#hc4a01595-cae9-4a72-a217-5d924770dd5d
column4: URL1 & URL2 (sections on "What you need to apply" from scraped_parking_permit_text.txt)

column2: What is a resident parking permit, and what are my benefits with it?
column3: With a parking permit, you can park in your permit area where paid parking is in force.
column4: URL 1 (How to apply for a resident parking permit)

column2: What's the specific emission standard required for petrol cars if I want a resident parking permit?
column3: For cars running on petrol or other non-diesel fuels, the emission standard must be 4 or higher.
column4: URL1 (Environmental requirements section)

column2: If I live in Weesp, do the same rules about parking permits for new-build projects apply to me?
column3: No, the rule stating that parking permits are not available for new-build projects does not apply to Weesp.
column4: URL4 ("Adressen waar geen parkeervergunning mogelijk is", Dutch section)

column2: How can I check online if my specific Amsterdam address is excluded from getting a parking permit?
column3: You can check if your address is excluded by using the 'Adreszoeker' (Address checker) for residents or companies available on the municipality's website.
column4: URL4 ("Adressen waar geen parkeervergunning mogelijk is", Dutch section)

column2: If my new address isn't public yet because it's a new construction, how can I find out if it's eligible for a parking permit?
column3: If your address is for a new construction and not yet public, it won't be in the 'Adreszoeker' yet. You can find these future addresses listed in an overview on the municipality's website, typically categorized by district (Stadsdeel).
column4: URL4 ("Adressen waar geen parkeervergunning mogelijk is", Dutch section)

column2: What are the specific office hours if I need to call the City of Amsterdam about my parking permit from within the Netherlands?
column3: You can call the City of Amsterdam at 14 020 from Monday to Friday, between 08:00 and 18:00.
column4: URL1 (Contact information section)

column2: If I'm applying for a resident parking permit by post and I have a car registered in my own name, do I still need to send a copy of my passport?
column3: Yes, if you apply by post for your own car, you need to send a copy of your passport or ID card. If your citizen service number (BSN) is on the back, you should copy both sides.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post for a residents’ parking permit" - What you need / Your own car)

column2: For a postal application, if my car's registration certificate isn't in the Latin alphabet, what do I need to provide?
column3: If your car's registration certificate is not in the Latin alphabet, you must send a translation with your postal application.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post for a residents’ parking permit" - What you need / Your own car)

column2: My employer leases my car, but I don't have a payslip yet because I just started. Can I submit my employment contract instead for the parking permit application?
column3: Yes, if your employer leases or provides the company car and you don't have a payslip yet (for example, because you just started), you can send a copy of your employment contract instead.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post..." - Lease car or rental car / Company car sections)

column2: If I own a company and the company car is registered in the company's name, are any extra documents needed for the resident parking permit application?
column3: If you are the owner of the business and the car's registration number is in the name of the company, no additional documents are usually needed for the resident parking permit application.
column4: URL1 & URL2 (sections for business owners with company-registered cars)

column2: If I apply for a parking permit by post, will I get a confirmation that my application was received?
column3: No, if you apply by post, you will not receive a confirmation of receipt for your application.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post..." - Next steps section)

column2: When does a parking permit obtained via postal application actually become valid?
column3: If you apply by post, your parking permit becomes valid only after you have paid the applicable charges. You will find more information about this in the letter you receive.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post..." - Next steps section)

column2: How long does it usually take for the municipality to process a change of vehicle registration number if the car is *not* registered in my name and I submitted the request before 4 PM?
column3: If the car is not registered in your name and the request to change the registration number is received before 16:00 (4 PM), it is typically processed on the same day.
column4: URL3 ("Changing the registration number..." - How long does a change take to process?)

column2: My car was stolen. How old can the police report be when I apply for a temporary change of registration on my parking permit?
column3: If your car was stolen, the police report you submit for a temporary change of registration on your parking permit should be a maximum of 1 month old.
column4: URL3 ("Changing the registration number..." - Temporary car due to theft)

column2: If I've temporarily changed my parking permit to a loaner car, do I need to do anything specific when I get my original car back within two weeks?
column3: Yes, if you can use your own car again within 2 weeks after a temporary change, you should use the online form to return the permit to your original vehicle registration number.
column4: URL3 ("Changing the registration number..." - Returning to your original vehicle registration number)

column2: For a Blue Zone waiver as a resident, what's the maximum number of waivers I can get for my address?
column3: As a resident, you can get a maximum of 2 Blue Zone waivers per address.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Bewoners section, Dutch)

column2: I'm a resident in a Blue Zone area with one car and my own private garage. Am I eligible for a Blue Zone waiver?
column3: No, if you are a resident in a Blue Zone with one car and you have your own garage or parking space, you are not eligible for a Blue Zone waiver.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Bewoners section, Dutch)

column2: If my company is located in a Blue Zone, how is the number of Blue Zone waivers it can receive calculated in relation to its number of employees?
column3: A company located in a Blue Zone can receive a maximum of 1 Blue Zone waiver per 5 employees.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Bedrijven section, Dutch)

column2: I receive informal care (mantelzorg) at my home in a Blue Zone. How many of my caregivers' car registration numbers can be listed on a single Blue Zone waiver?
column3: If you receive informal care (mantelzorg) in a Blue Zone, you can get 1 waiver for caregivers, and a maximum of 3 caregivers' registration numbers can be listed on it.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Bewoners die mantelzorg krijgen section, Dutch)

column2: What's the additional fee if I choose to pay for a Blue Zone waiver by invoice instead of iDEAL in 2025?
column3: If you choose to pay for a Blue Zone waiver by invoice (achteraf betalen) in 2025, there is an additional fee of €23.20.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Kosten section, Dutch)

column2: If my application for a Blue Zone waiver is rejected, do I still have to pay the application costs (leges)?
column3: Yes, you always have to pay the costs for processing your application (leges), even if your application for a Blue Zone waiver is rejected.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Kosten section, Dutch)

column2: I don't have a payslip yet, what can I do now? I have a company car
column3:
If you have a company car and don't have a payslip yet, you can send a copy of your employment contract instead. You will also need a statement from your employer, no older than 1 month, confirming that you are an employee of the company and the only employee permitted to drive the car.

If the car's registration is in the name of a company partner or director (and not the company itself), you would additionally need a copy of the passport or ID card of that owner.
column4: URL 2

column2: I'm a refugee from ukraine, how do I apply for these things
column3: Are you a Ukrainian refugee residing at a shelter? Or at an address where a parking permit is already in use? Please send the form to ua.parking@amsterdam.nl.
column4: URL 2

column2: Hey, I couldn't really find what I was looking for, can you provide me with a contact or phone number?
column3:
Yes, here is the contact information of the City of Amsterdam
Call us at 14 020
Monday to Friday from 08.00 to 18.00.
(From abroad: +31 20 624 1111)
column4: URL 2

column2: If I have a lease car that's not in my name (e.g., through my employer), what's the maximum age for the lease agreement or lease declaration I need to provide for the permit application?
column3: If you have a lease car not in your name, the lease or rental agreement with your employer's name should be a maximum of 1 year old, or the lease declaration with your employer's name should be a maximum of 1 month old.
column4: URL1 ("A lease car or a rental car" - If you have a rental car or lease car that is not in your name) & URL2 ("Lease car or rental car" - Your name is NOT on the contract)

column2: For a company car where the registration is in the employer's name, what's the maximum age for the employer's declaration statement?
column3: For a company car registered in your employer's name, the declaration from your employer (stating you are an employee and the only one permitted to drive the car) should be a maximum of 1 month old.
column4: URL1 ("Company car" - If the registration number is in the name of the employer) & URL2 ("Company car" - Licence plate... in the name of the employer)

column2: If the company car's registration is in the name of a partner or director of the company, what specific details from their passport or ID card need to be provided?
column3: If the company car's registration is in the name of a partner or director, you must scan and upload their passport or ID card. If their citizen service number (BSN) is on the back, then both sides need to be scanned/copied.
column4: URL1 ("Company car" - If the registration number is in the name of the partner or manager) & URL2 ("Company car" - Licence plate... in the name of company partner or director)

column2: If I apply online for a resident parking permit and the system needs more information from me, how will I be notified?
column3: If you apply online and more information is needed, the municipality will send you an email.
column4: URL1 ("Apply" section)

column2: What's the phone number to call the City of Amsterdam about parking permits if I'm calling from abroad?
column3: If you are calling from abroad about parking permits, the phone number for the City of Amsterdam is +31 20 624 1111.
column4: URL1 (Contact information section)

column2: Is there a specific email address mentioned for Ukrainian refugees to send their parking permit application forms if they reside at a shelter or an address with an existing permit?
column3: Yes, if you are a Ukrainian refugee residing at a shelter or at an address where a parking permit is already in use, you should send the application form to ua.parking@amsterdam.nl.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post for a residents’ parking permit" section)

column2: If I live in a building with a garage but no parking space has ever been available to me, what specific Dutch-named form or declaration do I need to prove this for a postal application?
column3: If you live in a building with a garage or car park and no parking space is, or has been in the past, available for you, you must prove this with the ‘geen eigen parkeerplaats’ (‘no own parking space’) declaration form.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post..." - Parking space at your residence)

column2: For a postal application with my own car, what is the RDW registration report, and when would I typically receive it?
column3: For a postal application with your own car, you need a copy of the car’s registration certificate or of the Netherlands Vehicle Authority (RDW) registration report. You typically receive the RDW registration report when you buy a car and register it in your name.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post..." - Your own car)

column2: If the registration of a company car is in the name of a company partner or director, do I still need an employer's statement confirming I'm the sole driver?
column3: Yes, if the company car's registration is in the name of a company partner or director, you still need a declaration from your employer (no older than 1 month) stating that you are an employee of the company and the only employee permitted to drive the car.
column4: URL1 ("Company car" - If the registration number is in the name of the partner or manager) & URL2 ("Company car" - Licence plate... in the name of company partner or director)

column2: If I submit a change of registration number online, and the vehicle is registered in my name, how quickly is it typically processed?
column3: If the vehicle registration is in your name, a change of registration number submitted online is processed immediately.
column4: URL3 ("Changing the registration number..." - How long does a change take to process?)

column2: If my car was stolen and I don't have the police report yet, can I use an email confirmation of the report for a temporary change of registration?
column3: Yes, if your car was stolen and the police report is not yet available, an email confirmation of the report can be used for a temporary change of registration.
column4: URL3 ("Changing the registration number..." - Temporary car due to theft)

column2: For which city districts (stadsdelen) in Amsterdam can one typically apply for a Blue Zone waiver?
column3: You can apply for a Blue Zone waiver in the city districts of Nieuw-West, Noord, Oost, and Zuidoost.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Zo werkt het section, Dutch)

column2: If I am a resident with two cars and no private parking, can I get a Blue Zone waiver, and if so, how many registration numbers can be on it?
column3: If you are a resident with 2 or more cars and no private parking, you can get 1 Blue Zone waiver. Two registration numbers will be put on this waiver, and you choose which car uses it at any given time.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Bewoners section, Dutch)

column2: For businesses in a Blue Zone, is the number of private parking spaces they own deducted from the total number of waivers they can get?
column3: Yes, for businesses in a Blue Zone, the number of their own parking spaces is deducted from the total number of waivers they can receive.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Bedrijven section, Dutch)

column2: What kind of organizations, apart from businesses, can apply for Blue Zone waivers based on their number of employees (e.g., 2 waivers per 5 employees)?
column3: Besides businesses, care institutions (zorginstellingen), educational institutions (onderwijsinstellingen), and police stations (politiebureaus) can apply for Blue Zone waivers based on their number of employees, typically receiving a maximum of 2 waivers per 5 employees.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Zorginstellingen, onderwijsinstellingen, politiebureaus section, Dutch)

column2: To get a Blue Zone waiver for a caregiver (mantelzorger), does the caregiver need to live outside my Blue Zone area?
column3: Yes, one of the conditions for a Blue Zone waiver for a caregiver (mantelzorger) is that your caregiver lives outside your Blue Zone area.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Voorwaarden / Bewoners die mantelzorg krijgen section, Dutch)

column2: For a Blue Zone waiver application, what specific document is needed to prove I am eligible for informal care (mantelzorg)?
column3: To prove you are eligible for informal care (mantelzorg) for a Blue Zone waiver, you need a recent declaration from an institution showing you are designated for such care. An indication for home care or a nursing home is also acceptable.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Aanvragen / Wat u nodig heeft section, Dutch)

column2: How long does it typically take to receive a Blue Zone waiver by post after applying?
column3: You will typically receive the Blue Zone waiver by post within 5 working days after applying.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Zo lang duurt het section, Dutch)

column2: If I no longer need my Blue Zone waiver before its 2-year validity ends, do I need to officially cancel it, and will I get a refund?
column3: No, you do not need to officially cancel your Blue Zone waiver; it expires automatically. You will not receive a refund if you stop using it before its validity ends.
column4: URL5 ("Ontheffing blauwe zone aanvragen" - Opzeggen niet nodig section, Dutch)

column2: In some parts of Amsterdam, what’s the maximum number of resident parking permits an address can get?
column3: A maximum of 2 permits per address.
column4: URL1 (from “In some parts of Amsterdam … maximum of 2 permits per address” section)

column2: I can’t use the garage in my building. Which document must I include with my permit application?
column3: The signed ‘Declaration of No Available Parking’ (‘geen eigen parkeerplaats’).
column4: URL1 (from “Parking space at your house” section)

column2: When I change the registration on a lease or rental car that’s in my name, what proof do I upload?
column3: A copy of the lease/rental agreement (≤ 1 year old) or a lease statement (≤ 1 month old).
column4: URL3 (from “Leased car in your name” section)

column2: What’s the official way to notifyAmsterdam of a change of vehicle registration number?
column3: Submit the change online with DigiD.
column4: URL3 (from “Let us know … online with your DigiD” paragraph)

column2: If the vehicle is registered in my own name, what extra documents are required for a registration-number change?
column3: None, no additional documents are needed.
column4: URL3 (from “Owner of the vehicle: No documents needed” line)

column2: My request to change the registration of a car not in my name arrived after 16:00. When will it be processed?
column3: On the next working day.

column2: I drive a car with foreign licence plates. What must I provide when changing the registration on my permit?
column3: A copy of the foreign registration certificate plus a translation.
column4: URL3 (from “Car with foreign registration” line)

column2: For an employer-leased car, which three documents are required to change its registration number?
column3: (1) Payslip ≤ 2 months old or employment contract; (2) employer’s statement ≤ 1 month old naming you sole driver; (3) lease agreement ≤ 1 year old or lease statement ≤ 1 month old.
column4: URL3 (from “Employer leases a car for you” block)

column2: For a company car in the employer’s name, how old can the employer’s declaration be?
column3: No more than 1 month old.

column2: The company car is in a partner/director’s name. Which ID must I upload?
column3: A passport or ID card of that owner (scan both sides if the BSN is on the back).

column2: After I’ve posted my paper application, within how many working days will the municipality respond by letter?

column2: What does that follow-up letter tell you?

column2: What appears on a Blue Zone waiver for residents?

column2: Where must you place the Blue Zone waiver once you receive it?

column2: Before applying for a waiver in Nieuw-West or Zuidoost, what page should you check first?
column3: The page ‘Betaald parkeren in Nieuw-West’ or ‘Betaald parkeren in Zuidoost’.

column2: When a company applies for a Blue Zone waiver, which document must show its number of employees?

column2: If you’re a resident with a lease car requesting a Blue Zone waiver, what document is required?

column2: For a caregiver’s (mantelzorger) Blue Zone waiver where the caregiver drives a company car, what extra proof is needed?

column2: With a caregiver waiver, how many caregivers can park at once?

column2: How can I avoid the € 23.20 surcharge when paying for a Blue Zone waiver?
column3: By paying immediately with iDEAL.
column4: URL5 (from “U betaalt … € 23,20 extra als u achteraf wilt betalen … direct betalen met iDEAL … geen extra kosten”)

column2: After an online permit application, what do you receive if you immediately qualify?
column3: Its shown immediately on the screen.
column4: URL1 (from “you will immediately receive an e-mail with the confirmation”)

column2: My car was stolen and the police report isn’t ready. Can I still request a temporary registration change?
column3: Yes, an email confirmation of the report is acceptable.
column4: URL3 (from “If police report is not yet available, an email confirmation …”)

column2: When changing a registration number, what is the maximum age of an acceptable payslip?
column3: The payslip must be no more than 2 months old.
column4: URL3 (from “Payslip (max. 2 months old) …”)

column2: During an online application, which details are automatically checked by the system?
column3: Your address and the vehicle registration number.
column4: URL1 (from “we automatically check details such as your address and registration number”)

column2: Are there areas in Amsterdam where I'd apply for a Blue Zone waiver instead of a regular resident parking permit, and which areas are these?
column3: Yes, in some areas, a Blue Zone waiver (ontheffing) is used instead of a standard resident parking permit. You can typically apply for these Blue Zone waivers in the city districts of Nieuw-West, Noord, Oost, and Zuidoost.
column4: URL1 (from “instead of a parking permit, you can apply for a waiver for the blue zone” sentence)

column2: For care institutions, schools or police stations, are their own parking spaces deducted from the waivers they can receive?
column3: Yes, the number of private spaces is subtracted (-) from the total waivers allowed.
column4: URL5 (from “Wij trekken het aantal eigen parkeerplaatsen … af van het totale aantal ontheffingen”)

column2: My car was stolen, what should I do in my case?
column3: If your car is stolen, apply for a temporary change of registration number on your resident parking permit. Send a police report no older than 1 month that lists the stolen car’s registration number. If the report is not yet available, an e-mail confirmation of the report is acceptable. When your own car is back in use (for example, within 2 weeks), use the online form to restore the permit to your original registration number.
column4: URL3 (from “Changing the registration number…” – “Temporary car due to theft” and “Returning to your original vehicle registration number” sections)

column2: Do you have amsterdam tourist information?
column3: Yes, visit iamsterdam.com – the city’s “To do in Amsterdam” portal with information about tourism, hotels, cultural events and going out.
column4: URL 1 (How to apply for a resident parking permit)

column2: Can I apply for a resident parking permit if I live in a hotel?
column3: Only applicable if the hotel address is your registered BRP address. A resident permit is issued solely to people who are registered at the address for which they apply.
column4: URL1 (from “You must meet these conditions” – “You are registered at the address for which you are applying for the permit” section)

column2: I’m moving to another part of Amsterdam. What must I do with my current resident parking permit?
column3: You must cancel the permit attached to your old address and then apply for a new permit for your new address.
column4: URL 6 (from “Parking permit transfer when moving house” – first paragraph)

column2: My company is relocating. Can we simply transfer our business parking permitto the new address?
column3: No, just like residents, a business must terminate the permit for the old address and apply for a new oneat the new premises.
column4: URL 6 (from “Parking permit transfer when moving house” – first paragraph)

column2: What are the basic conditions for getting a parking permit at my new address after moving?
column3: You must (1) be officially registered at the new address, (2) the area must have paid parking, and (3) you may not have a private parking space there. New-build properties are excluded from permits.
column4: URL 6 (from “Conditions” list)

column2: Do I need to be registered at my new address beforeapplying for the permit there?
column3: Yes. Registration at the new address is a prerequisite for the new permit.
column4: URL 6 (from “You are registered at your new address…” bullet)

column2: If there is paid parking in my new street but I have my own garage, can I still get a permit?
column3: No. Having a private parking space at the new address disqualifies you from a resident or business permit there.
column4: URL 6 (from “You do not have a parking space at your new address” bullet)

column2: Can I receive a parking permit for a new-build property I’m moving into?
column3: No. The city does not issue resident or business permits for new-build properties.
column4: URL 6 (from “Please note: you cannot get a parking permit for new-build properties.”)

column2: I was already on a waiting list for a permit. What happens to my waiting-list place when I move?
column3: Your place transfers: you are put on the waiting list of the new permit area using your original application date (or the date you joined the waiting list).
column4: URL 6 (from “On the waiting list” section)

column2: Will I lose my seniority on the waiting list if the new area also has a list?
column3: No. Your original application or registration date is kept, so your seniority is preserved.
column4: URL 6 (from “will be placed on the waiting list … based on the original application date”)

column2: What are the practical steps to obtain a permit when I move house?
column3: First terminate your existing permit, then apply for a new permit, choose either the resident-permit or business-permit application process, depending on your situation.
column4: URL 6 (from “How to apply” section)

column2: Do the move-house rules apply to both residents and businesses, or only to residents?
column3: They apply to both resident and business permits, as well as to anyone on a waiting list for either.
column4: URL 6 (from “This applies to parking permits for both residents and businesses, or your position on the waiting list…”)

column2: How many parking permits can I have?
column3: In Amsterdam you can never have more than two resident parking permits linked to a single address. Only some neighbourhoods allow the full maximum of 2 permits per address; in other areas the limit is lower. Any private parking space you own (or could have rented/bought) is deducted from that number.
column4: URL1 (from “In some parts of Amsterdam you are able to apply for a maximum of 2 permits per address” rule)

column2: If my employer leases my car, and I provide my employment contract instead of a payslip, does the employer's statement still need to confirm I'm the only employee permitted to drive it?
column3: Yes, even if you provide an employment contract instead of a payslip (e.g., if you just started), you still need a statement from your employer, no older than 1 month, stating that you are an employee of the company and that you are the only employee permitted to drive the car.
column4: URL2 ("Applying by post..." - Lease car or rental car / Your name is NOT on the contract)
